items.title: The Constitution 131st Amendment Bill 2026
items.whyInNews: The Union government has introduced a legislative package consisting of three pivotal Bills to restructure India’s parliamentary landscape, expand representation, and implement the long-awaited women’s reservation on Friday, 17 April 2026. The Bills include The Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026, The Delimitation Bill, 2026, and The Union Territories Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026.
items.summary: The Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026, provides the constitutional framework for expanding the legislature and lifting previous freezes on seat adjustments, increasing the maximum strength of the Lok Sabha from 550 to 850 members. The Delimitation Bill, 2026, creates the statutory mechanism and the body responsible for executing the redrawing of constituencies, while The Union Territories Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026, extends the 33% women’s reservation and delimitation overhaul to Delhi, Jammu & Kashmir, and Puducherry. The Bills aim to ensure equal representation and implement women's reservation in the 2029 elections.
keyPoints: The Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026, increases the maximum strength of the Lok Sabha from 550 to 850 members
The Delimitation Bill, 2026, establishes a new Delimitation Commission chaired by a Supreme Court judge
The Union Territories Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026, extends the 33% women’s reservation to Delhi, Jammu & Kashmir, and Puducherry
The Bills require a special majority in Parliament and ratification by at least half of the States
importantPoints: Article 81 of the Constitution will be amended to increase the maximum strength of the Lok Sabha
The Delimitation Commission will be responsible for redrawing territorial constituencies based on the 2011 Census
The 33% women’s reservation will be implemented in the 2029 elections
The Bills aim to ensure equal representation and increase women's participation in politics
The legislation will have a significant impact on India's parliamentary landscape and representation
items.examRelevance: This topic falls under the subject of Polity and is relevant for SSC students as it deals with constitutional amendments, delimitation, and women's reservation, which are important concepts in Indian politics
